sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Janus Henderson B-BBB CLO ETF (JBBB) stock price, news, quote and history
feed.summary: This page provides detailed information on the Janus Henderson B-BBB CLO ETF (JBBB), including its current stock price, historical data, and performance overview. The ETF focuses on securitized bonds and invests primarily in U.S. dollar-denominated CLOs, with a limit of 15% in CLOs rated below investment grade. It also lists related news and other ETFs that investors might consider.

feed.title: US ends probe into Tesla remote driving feature after software updates
feed.summary: The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has closed its investigation into Tesla's "Actually Smart Summon" feature, which allows remote vehicle movement. The probe, covering nearly 2.6 million vehicles, concluded that the feature was linked primarily to low-speed incidents with minor property damage and no injuries or fatalities. Tesla has addressed these issues through software updates improving obstacle detection and vehicle response.

feed.title: Roth MKM Maintains Diamondback Energy(FANG.US) With Buy Rating, Raises Target Price to $193.88
feed.summary: Roth MKM has reiterated its Buy rating on Diamondback Energy (FANG.US) and increased its price target to $193.88. This indicates a positive outlook from the firm regarding the company's stock performance.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper stock hits 52-week low at 25.03 USD
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) stock has hit a 52-week low at $25.03, representing a 22.6% decline over the past year and an 8.1% year-to-date loss. Despite this, InvestingPro analysis identifies the stock as undervalued, with a P/E ratio of 16.54 and a PEG ratio of 0.36. The company recently reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, surpassing expectations, and completed a significant acquisition of JDE Peet’s shares, prompting UBS to raise its price target to $36 with a Buy rating.

feed.title: Hasbro opens distribution hub to cut costs, speed deliveries
feed.summary: Hasbro has launched a new 600,000-square-foot distribution center in Midway, Georgia, aimed at significantly reducing costs and improving fulfillment efficiency. The facility is expected to generate $8 million in annual productivity savings by lowering overhead, transportation, and 3PL costs through a consolidation of its U.S. distribution network. This strategic move also enhances resilience and speeds up direct-to-consumer deliveries by two to three days, supporting both brick-and-mortar and online operations.

feed.title: S&P Global Inc. stock: Fresh Mobility Spin-Off Moves Signal Big Changes Ahead
feed.summary: S&P Global Inc. is preparing for a mid-2026 spin-off of its Mobility unit, marked by the appointment of Renato Negro as Chief Accounting Officer. This strategic move aims to create a pure-play mobility data entity while allowing the core S&P Global business to focus on financial services. Analysts maintain a strong buy consensus, suggesting significant upside potential for investors from this re-organization.

feed.title: Recycling volumes aren’t vanishing — they’re just harder to track
feed.summary: The article discusses how recycling volumes are becoming more difficult to track, rather than disappearing. It highlights concerns within the industry about the transparency and traceability of recycled materials. The piece suggests that this tracking difficulty poses challenges for understanding the true state of recycling efforts.

feed.title: Is Dividend Hike And Recent Earnings Momentum Altering The Investment Case For Independent Bank (INDB)?
feed.summary: Independent Bank (INDB) recently increased its quarterly dividend by 8.5% and reported strong Q4 2025 earnings and loan growth, indicating a focus on shareholder returns and operating momentum. While these factors support the bank's income-focused investment narrative and financial flexibility, the core investment case remains centered on managing earnings resilience and credit quality, especially given its concentrated commercial real estate exposure. The increased dividend yield now surpasses industry averages, but potential credit costs and real estate stress are key risks to monitor.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on Seagate Technology to $582 From $468, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for Seagate Technology (STX) to $582 from $468, while reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook for the company. The article also provides a brief overview of Seagate's business, which includes mass data storage systems, internal hard drives, and external storage solutions.

feed.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Price Target on ConocoPhillips to $144 From $138, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips (COP) to $144 from $138, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the research firm regarding the oil and gas exploration and production company. ConocoPhillips specializes in crude oil, natural gas, and liquefied natural gas production, with major operations in the United States and other international regions.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Adjusts Agilent Technologies Price Target to $140 From $160, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has revised its price target for Agilent Technologies (NYSE: A) to $140, down from the previous $160, while reiterating an Outperform rating for the stock. This adjustment comes amidst recent news regarding Agilent's activities, including FDA approvals for diagnostic tools and significant acquisition deals. Agilent Technologies specializes in measurement instruments for various sectors, primarily life sciences, electronics, and chemistry.

feed.title: Inovio Pharmaceuticals Announces New Public Equity Offering
feed.summary: Inovio Pharmaceuticals has announced a public offering of 12.5 million shares and accompanying Series A and B warrants at $1.40 per share and warrants. This deal, with an option for underwriters to purchase additional shares, is expected to generate approximately $16 million in net proceeds for the company. The financing aims to support Inovio's DNA medicine programs and bolster its financial standing, especially within the competitive biotech sector.

feed.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Price Target on Phillips 66 to $193 From $187, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has increased its price target for Phillips 66 (PSX) to $193 from $187, while reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the research firm regarding the oil and gas refining and marketing company. The article also briefly lists other recent analyst adjustments and company news.

feed.title: Raymond James cuts Phreesia stock price target on pharma ad headwinds
feed.summary: Raymond James has lowered its price target for Phreesia Inc. (NYSE:PHR) to $16 from $25, citing headwinds in pharmaceutical advertising despite the company beating Q4 fiscal 2026 revenue and adjusted EBITDA estimates. Phreesia also reduced its fiscal 2027 revenue guidance by about 7% due to lower visibility in pharma ad spending, although it maintained its adjusted EBITDA guidance by planning cost controls through AI. Other analysts, including Citizens and BMO Capital, have also adjusted their ratings and price targets following Phreesia's updated outlook.

feed.title: Blackstone-Backed Data Center Firm QTS Kicks Off Bond Sale
feed.summary: Blackstone-backed QTS has launched the sale of a 10-year investment-grade green bond. This move supports the firm's construction of a significant data center in Georgia. Initial price discussions indicate a spread of approximately 1.625 percentage points over Treasuries.

feed.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Price Target on Coterra Energy to $46 From $45, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has increased its price target for Coterra Energy (NYSE: CTRA) shares to $46 from $45, while reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook for the oil and natural gas exploration and production company. Coterra Energy specializes in basins across North America, including the Appalachian, Gulf Coast, Rocky Mountains, Anadarko, and western Canada regions.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on Martin Marietta Materials to $664 From $702, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has adjusted its price target for Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) to $664, down from $702, while reiterating an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This move comes as other analysts have also revised their price targets for the construction materials company. The article also provides a brief overview of Martin Marietta Materials' business, highlighting its position as America's second-largest producer of granulates and construction materials.

feed.title: Yum Brands appoints former Marriott International CFO to Board
feed.summary: Yum Brands Inc. has appointed Kathleen "Leeny" K. Oberg, former CFO and executive vice president of Marriott International Inc., to its Board of Directors. Oberg brings extensive experience in driving growth and financial discipline from her nearly three decades at Marriott, including a decade as CFO. Her appointment is expected to help guide Yum Brands' global expansion and strategy.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on Assurant to $240 From $248, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Assurant (AIZ) to $240 from $248, while retaining an Equalweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised valuation for the insurance group. Assurant operates in insurance-credit and specialty property insurance sectors.

feed.title: BlackRock files for Nasdaq-100 fund, expanding competition with Invesco
feed.summary: BlackRock has filed for a new iShares Nasdaq-100 ETF, ticker "IQQ," challenging Invesco's dominant QQQ Trust ETF. The new fund aims to expand access to the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 index, which includes 100 of the largest non-financial companies listed on Nasdaq like Nvidia and Apple. This move introduces more competition into a market historically controlled by a few key players.

feed.title: Constellation Energy Has Fallen 34% From Its Peak. Could 2026 Be the Turning Point?
feed.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) has seen a 34% decline from its October 2025 peak, reaching $272.82 by April 2, 2026, after its 2026 Business and Earnings Outlook failed to meet high market expectations, particularly regarding new hyperscaler contracts. Despite this, TIKR's mid-case model suggests a potential 77.6% total return by 2030, driven by the company's significant uncontracted clean nuclear generation capacity and strategic capital deployment, including share repurchases and dividends. The article highlights that the underlying business remains strong, with the stock's current valuation presenting a potential opportunity given its strategic assets and growth targets.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Adjusts Mettler-Toledo International PT to $1,425 From $1,515, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has adjusted its price target for Mettler-Toledo International (MTD) to $1,425, down from $1,515, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after the company's recent Q4 2025 earnings report, which showed increased adjusted earnings and revenue, leading to a raised 2026 EPS guidance. Mettler-Toledo specializes in precision instruments for laboratory, industrial, and food distribution sectors globally.

feed.title: TalentAlly, a Subsidiary of Professional Diversity Network, Inc., Launches Next-Generation Career Fair Hiring Platform Powered by ByteCompute.ai
feed.summary: TalentAlly, a subsidiary of Professional Diversity Network, Inc. (Nasdaq: IPDN), has launched a new career fair hiring platform powered by ByteCompute.ai. This platform aims to improve candidate engagement, recruiter efficiency, and hiring outcomes for virtual, in-person, and hybrid career events. It offers features like pre-event candidate targeting, real-time communication, structured candidate flow, advanced analytics, and an enhanced candidate experience.

feed.title: McCormick (MKC) CEO Foley receives 49.799 phantom stock units in savings plan
feed.summary: McCormick & Co. Chairman, President & CEO Brendan M. Foley received 49.799 shares of phantom stock on April 2, 2026, as part of a Non Qualified Retirement Savings Plan. This award, valued at $48.85 per share, increases his indirect holdings of phantom stock to 13,683.983 shares. This transaction is considered a routine compensation entry rather than an open-market trade, as his direct common stock holdings remain unchanged.

feed.title: Bull of the Day: Imperial Oil Limited (IMO)
feed.summary: Imperial Oil Limited (IMO), a leading Canadian integrated oil and gas company, has reached a five-year high in share price due to rising oil prices spurred by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The company has consistently outperformed earnings expectations, reporting its fifteenth consecutive quarter of exceeding estimates. Analysts have revised their forecasts upward, anticipating continued strong performance, and Imperial Oil maintains a strong commitment to shareholders with over a century of dividend payments.

feed.title: PayPay (NASDAQ:PAYP) Now Covered by Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group initiated coverage on PayPay (NASDAQ:PAYP) with a "Buy" rating and a $28.00 price target, suggesting a 33% upside. Several other firms, including Benchmark, Bank of America, Wolfe Research, and Morgan Stanley, also recently began coverage, resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $25.44. PayPay recently reported $0.18 EPS on $636.46 million in quarterly revenue.

feed.title: How AI Is Already Reshaping Cancer Care
feed.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) is already significantly transforming cancer care, particularly in streamlining clinical trial processes and drug discovery. Pfizer, through experts like Jeanine Bortel and Tamara Mansfeld, is utilizing AI to identify eligible patients for trials more quickly, thereby broadening access to potentially life-changing treatments and accelerating breakthroughs in oncology research. This use of AI, including machine learning and large language models, is improving efficiency, precision, and the overall speed of developing new cancer therapies.

feed.title: The AB Acceleration: Scaling Active Expertise in the ETF Era
feed.summary: AllianceBernstein (AB) has rapidly grown into a significant provider of actively managed ETFs, accumulating over $16 billion in assets since launching its initial suite in 2022. The firm specializes in fixed income, international quality equities, and defined-outcome strategies, successfully converting legacy mutual funds into more liquid ETF wrappers to meet investor demand for risk management and broader accessibility. This strategic shift has resulted in substantial inflows and several ETFs surpassing the $1 billion asset milestone.

feed.title: Is Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW) is presented as a strong cybersecurity stock with accelerating growth, particularly in its Next-Generation Security (NGS) business. Despite a previous depreciation in stock price, a bullish thesis highlights robust Q2 FY26 performance, strategic acquisitions, and positive future guidance. The article notes PANW's high-growth, high-margin engine and AI initiatives, although market valuation reflects optimism and execution of growth remains key.

feed.title: JBT Marel Has Fallen 27% From Its Highs. Is the Selloff a Buying Opportunity?
feed.summary: JBT Marel (JBTM) has experienced a 27% drop from its 52-week high, leading to questions about whether the selloff presents a buying opportunity. The company faces challenges such as projected tariff headwinds and integration complexity, but bulls are optimistic about the discounted integration story in a resilient market, backed by strong analyst targets and synergy realization. Investors are advised to watch the adjusted EBITDA margin when Q1 2026 results are reported, as it will be a key indicator for the company's future performance.

feed.title: Top Ten Payments Companies Processed $11 Trillion in 2025 Payment Card Volume
feed.summary: TSG (The Strawhecker Group) announced new rankings for payments companies, revealing that the top ten processed $11 trillion in payment card volume in 2025, with the top five handling nearly $9 trillion. Global Payments secured the top spot after acquiring Worldpay, processing close to $2.8 trillion, while Stripe entered the top 5 for the first time with over $900 billion in U.S. processing volume. These findings are featured in TSG’s 2026 Directory of U.S. Merchant Acquirers, highlighting key industry trends and market shares.

feed.title: IonQ vs. Rigetti Computing: Which Quantum Computing Stock Has the Edge Now?
feed.summary: Investing in quantum computing involves high risk and reward, with pure-play companies like IonQ and Rigetti Computing offering greater upside potential than diversified tech giants. IonQ leads in accuracy with its trapped-ion technology, while Rigetti focuses on speed with superconducting systems. The article suggests diversifying investments across multiple pure-play companies or through an ETF to mitigate risk in this speculative market.

feed.title: One in 3 Americans now makes daily money trade-offs, KeyBank finds
feed.summary: A new KeyBank poll reveals that 33% of Americans make financial trade-offs daily, and 31% do so weekly, with 88% having made at least one significant financial adjustment due to economic pressures. Despite a drop in overall optimism, consumers are employing strategies like switching to cheaper brands and cutting subscriptions, while KeyBank advises proactive planning and leveraging banking relationships for financial stability. Key financial concerns include grocery prices, housing costs, and healthcare expenses.

feed.title: Why Booking Holdings’ Recent Selloff May Be Overdone
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) stock has experienced a significant 20% selloff this year, driven by AI concerns, market cooling, and geopolitical uncertainty, despite strong underlying business performance including 13% revenue growth and $9.1 billion in free cash flow in 2025. The company's valuation, especially its price-to-free cash flow, appears reasonable after the pullback, and its capital-light model and strong cash generation capabilities suggest the selloff may be exaggerated. AI is seen as both a risk and an opportunity, with Booking well-positioned to leverage it due to its extensive data and international market reach.

feed.title: ICE Mortgage Monitor: Early Spring Housing Market Shows Firmer Prices Amid Affordability Reset and Inventory Growth
feed.summary: The April 2026 ICE Mortgage Monitor report indicates that the early spring housing market experienced stronger conditions, driven by improved affordability and increasing inventory, despite a recent rise in interest rates. While mortgage rates saw an uptick, affordability remains better than a year ago in most markets. The report highlights that home price growth is modest but firming, and inventory is recovering yet still below pre-pandemic levels, leading to a gradually normalizing but uneven market.

feed.title: Inspira Technologies (IINN) pivots to quantum connectivity, plans QTREX rebrand
feed.summary: Inspira Technologies (IINN) is making a strategic pivot into the quantum computing sector, focusing on solving connectivity bottlenecks in dilution cryostats using its acquired additive manufactured electronics (AME) platform. While redirecting its AME technology to quantum solutions, Inspira will continue to advance its medical technology portfolio under a dedicated unit. The company plans to seek shareholder approval to change its name to QTREX Ltd. to reflect this new strategic direction.

feed.title: May 6 webcast: CVS Health to detail first-quarter 2026 results
feed.summary: CVS Health (NYSE: CVS) announced it will hold its first-quarter 2026 earnings conference call on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET. An audio webcast will be available live and archived for one year on the company's investor relations website. Historically, similar pre-announcements have resulted in modest stock price movements of around 0.37%.

feed.title: GroceryTech 2026 Is the Executive Destination for Kroger, Ahold Delhaize USA, Albertsons, Dollar General and More
feed.summary: GroceryTech 2026, scheduled for May 12-14 in Charlotte, N.C., is set to gather senior grocery leaders from major retailers like Kroger, Ahold Delhaize USA, Albertsons, and Dollar General. The event aims to explore how grocers are navigating industry changes, making investment decisions, and driving performance through themes like AI, improved store execution, unified retail media strategies, and robust data foundations. It will feature an executive speaker lineup and limited attendance to ensure high-value discussions and networking.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Bets Famous ‘Hilltop’ Jingle Still Has Fizz With Modern Crowds
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is reintroducing a modernized version of its iconic 1971 "I'd like to buy the world a Coke" jingle through a new ad set to debut during the NCAA March Madness championship game. The campaign aims to associate Coca-Cola with America's 250th birthday, focusing on themes of American harmony rather than global peace. This move leverages the brand's extensive advertising archives to resonate with contemporary audiences and stand out in today's diverse media landscape.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace (NYSE: HWM) completes $1.8B cash acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (NYSE: HWM) has successfully completed its previously announced acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing, LLC from Stanley Black & Decker for approximately $1.8 billion in cash. The transaction closed on April 6, 2026, marking a significant strategic move for Howmet Aerospace to expand its portfolio. This 8-K filing details the completion of the deal and highlights key financial aspects and terms of the acquisition.

feed.title: ICE Mortgage Monitor: Early Spring Housing Market Shows Firmer Prices Amid Affordability Reset and Inventory Growth
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE) released its April 2026 Mortgage Monitor report, indicating a stronger spring housing market driven by improved affordability and increasing inventory, despite a recent rise in interest rates. While home price growth remains modest at 0.4% annually, early spring saw the firmest monthly gains in nearly a year. Affordability has improved year-over-year, but higher rates have significantly reduced refinance incentives, and inventory, though recovering, is still below pre-pandemic levels.

feed.title: $1.8B CAM sale to cut debt at Stanley Black & Decker (NYSE: SWK)
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker has completed the sale of its Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing (CAM) business to Howmet Aerospace for approximately $1.8 billion in cash. The company expects to receive about $1.57 billion in net proceeds after taxes and fees, which it plans to use to significantly reduce its debt. This divestiture is part of a strategy to focus on core Tools and Outdoor businesses and achieve a target leverage ratio of around 2.5 times net debt to adjusted EBITDA by year end, enabling more dynamic capital allocation.

feed.title: Is Extra Space Storage Inc. (EXR) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: Extra Space Storage Inc. (EXR) is presented as a strong investment potential due to its position as the largest self-storage operator in the U.S. and its capital-light growth strategy. The company benefits from consolidating a fragmented industry, leveraging technology, and offering inflation protection through month-to-month leases. Despite these advantages, EXR is not among the 40 most popular stocks for hedge funds, with a decrease in hedge fund portfolios holding EXR in the last quarter.

feed.title: Online tutoring firm Nerdy hires ex-JLL, AWS finance leader
feed.summary: Nerdy Inc. has appointed Atul Bagga as its new Chief Financial Officer, effective April 6, 2026. Bagga brings extensive experience from his previous roles at JLL Technologies and Amazon Web Services. This strategic hire aims to support Nerdy's continued growth, margin expansion, and value creation, building on the company's recent achievement of adjusted EBITDA profitability in late 2025 and its projected revenue of $180-$190 million for 2026.

feed.title: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) Raised to Buy at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group upgraded Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) from a "hold" to a "buy" rating, setting an $85 price target, representing a potential 26.28% upside. This upgrade follows Kratos's strong quarterly performance, where it surpassed EPS and revenue expectations. Despite significant insider selling, institutional investors and hedge funds maintain substantial ownership in the company.

feed.title: State Street (NYSE:STT) Rating Increased to Neutral at Bank of America
feed.summary: Bank of America upgraded State Street (NYSE:STT) from "underperform" to "neutral" with a $143 price target, signaling a potential 10.99% upside. This follows State Street's beat on quarterly earnings expectations, reporting EPS of $2.97 and revenue of $3.67 billion. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with several other firms also issuing positive recommendations.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) Given Hold Rating at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has reiterated a "hold" rating on Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) with a $61.00 price target, suggesting a potential upside of 12.20%. This comes after Las Vegas Sands beat Q4 2025 earnings and revenue expectations, reporting $0.85 EPS and $3.65 billion in revenue. Despite a recent sale of shares by CEO Patrick Dumont, the stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average price target of $67.86.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Raises Dividend And Reinforces Income Stability
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company announced its 64th consecutive annual dividend increase of 3.9%, raising the quarterly payout from $0.51 to $0.53. This increase, with shares trading near $75.71 on March 27, results in a forward yield of approximately 2.68% and signals the company's steady cash flow and appeal to income investors. The ex-dividend date is March 13 with payout on April 1.

feed.title: Hologic CEO Steve MacMillan To Retire
feed.summary: Hologic, Inc. announced that CEO Steve MacMillan will retire following the company's go-private transaction with Blackstone and TPG, expected to close around April 7, 2026. MacMillan's 12-plus year tenure saw significant growth in revenue, earnings per share, and stock price, along with the company's response to the COVID pandemic. The board and MacMillan expressed gratitude for his leadership, and a new CEO is expected to be announced upon the transaction's close.

feed.title: NTSB Finds Overreliance on ADAS to Blame for Ford BlueCruise Crashes
feed.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) has attributed drivers' overreliance on automated systems to two fatal 2024 crashes involving Ford's BlueCruise system, where vehicles failed to stop for stationary objects. The NTSB highlighted issues with insufficient data recording, ineffective driver monitoring, and a lack of federal performance standards for Level 2 automation systems. Recommendations have been issued to the U.S. Department of Transportation, NHTSA, and Ford to implement stronger safety guidelines, improve crash data recording, and enhance driver monitoring and system configurations.
